subject: Product Management
title: Dropbox: The Video MVP
description: A quick, practical guide to Dropbox: The Video MVP.

hook.text: Drew Houston couldn't explain Dropbox to investors. Syncing files across computers sounded boring. So he made a video.

buildup.text: The three-minute demo video showed Dropbox working seamlessly. No jargon — just a person syncing files effortlessly.

discovery.text: The video went viral on Hacker News. The waitlist jumped from 5,000 to 75,000 users overnight.

twist.text: The product barely worked at that point. But the demand signal was undeniable — people desperately wanted this.

climax.text: Houston used that waitlist as proof of PMF to raise funding and build the real product. The video was the MVP.

punchline.text: A three-minute video proved PMF before the product existed.

quiz.question: How did Dropbox validate product-market fit early?
options: A demo video that grew the waitlist from 5,000 to 75,000 overnight
By launching a fully built product to the public
